reasons: Kaja's suppression gives Dyrroth a stable target for his close-range burst sequence.
Dyrroth can use the catch window to apply damage before the target repositions.

proof.summary: Kaja locks and drags the target, while Dyrroth follows with close-range burst.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches a squishy or damaged target.
Dyrroth is close enough to follow immediately.
failureCases: Dyrroth is too far from the target.
The enemy peels Dyrroth after the suppression ends.

reasons: Franco can isolate a target with hook or suppression so Dyrroth can collapse with close-range damage.
Dyrroth's burst helps convert Franco's catch into a kill threat.

proof.summary: Franco creates the catch window, and Dyrroth supplies the damage needed to finish the target.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a priority target near Dyrroth.
Dyrroth saves his main damage for the catch.
failureCases: Dyrroth commits after the target has already escaped.

reasons: Angela helps Dyrroth stay alive while he commits into melee range for burst trades.
The attached shield and sustain let Dyrroth continue fighting after his first engage.

proof.summary: Dyrroth's damage requires close contact, and Angela helps him survive that commit.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches as Dyrroth starts the dive.
Dyrroth targets an enemy he can realistically finish.
failureCases: Dyrroth overextends into layered crowd control.
Angela attaches after Dyrroth is already low.
The enemy disengages and wastes Dyrroth's cooldowns.

reasons: Floryn can sustain Dyrroth during aggressive skirmishes even when he fights away from the healer.
Global healing helps Dyrroth continue trading after committing into melee range.

proof.summary: Dyrroth benefits from extra sustain during aggressive trades, and Floryn can heal globally.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth baits damage before Floryn casts Bloom.
Dyrroth still has mobility or damage cooldowns after receiving the heal.
failureCases: Dyrroth is burst down before healing arrives.
Dyrroth commits into too many enemies without follow-up.

reasons: Carmilla's linked-target setup can make Dyrroth's focused damage more valuable when enemies group around the target.
Dyrroth can focus one enemy while Carmilla adds teamfight pressure to nearby enemies.

proof.summary: Dyrroth focuses one enemy, while Carmilla's link can punish nearby enemies through linked damage and control interaction.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla links enemies before Dyrroth commits.
Dyrroth attacks a target near other linked enemies.
failureCases: Dyrroth fights an isolated target while Carmilla's link misses.
The enemy spreads out before Dyrroth commits.
Dyrroth is peeled before he can finish the target.
